title: Your Pregnancy Journey Begins
baby_development: Pregnancy is counted from the first day of your last menstrual period.
Your body is preparing an egg for ovulation.
mother_changes: Normal menstrual cycle may still be occurring.
Healthy habits are important before conception.
nutrition_tip: Begin taking folic acid (400–800 mcg daily) if planning pregnancy.
exercise_tip: Continue regular physical activity if healthy.
fun_fact: Pregnancy weeks are counted before fertilization actually occurs.
warning_signs: Seek medical advice if you have severe pelvic pain or unusually heavy bleeding.

title: Ovulation Approaches
baby_development: Fertilization may occur if sperm meets the egg.
mother_changes: Hormones prepare the uterus for implantation.
nutrition_tip: Eat balanced meals rich in leafy vegetables.
exercise_tip: Maintain moderate physical activity.
fun_fact: The baby's genetic characteristics are determined at fertilization.

title: A New Life Begins
baby_development: The fertilized egg becomes a blastocyst.
mother_changes: Most women have no noticeable symptoms.
nutrition_tip: Continue folic acid daily.
exercise_tip: Walking is encouraged.
fun_fact: Your baby's DNA is already completely unique.

title: Tiny but Growing
nutrition_tip: Drink plenty of water.
exercise_tip: Light walking is beneficial.
weekly_milestone: Positive pregnancy test for many mothers.
fun_fact: Your baby's future brain and spinal cord have started developing.
warning_signs: Severe abdominal pain requires medical attention.

title: The Heart Starts Developing
nutrition_tip: Eat smaller meals to reduce nausea.
exercise_tip: Gentle stretching can help.
fun_fact: The heart will soon begin beating.
warning_signs: Persistent vomiting causing dehydration requires medical review.

title: Heartbeat Begins
baby_development: Heartbeat can often be detected by ultrasound.
nutrition_tip: Stay hydrated throughout the day.
exercise_tip: Short walks are recommended if comfortable.
fun_fact: The heart beats much faster than an adult's.
warning_signs: Heavy bleeding or severe cramping requires urgent evaluation.

title: Rapid Brain Growth
nutrition_tip: Include protein-rich foods.
exercise_tip: Prenatal stretching is beneficial.
fun_fact: Millions of brain cells are forming every minute.
warning_signs: Persistent severe pain should be assessed.

title: Tiny Fingers Form
nutrition_tip: Iron-rich foods support healthy blood production.
exercise_tip: Continue gentle activity.
fun_fact: Although baby moves, you cannot feel it yet.

title: Now Officially a Fetus
nutrition_tip: Increase dietary fiber.
exercise_tip: Walking helps digestion.
fun_fact: Nearly all major organs have started developing.

title: Tiny Nails Appear
nutrition_tip: Eat calcium-rich foods.
exercise_tip: Prenatal yoga may be suitable if recommended by your healthcare provider.
fun_fact: Baby can already swallow small amounts of amniotic fluid.
warning_signs: Contact your healthcare provider if you have severe bleeding, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting.

title: Baby is Becoming More Active
nutrition_tip: Eat calcium-rich foods to support bone development.
exercise_tip: Gentle walking and stretching are beneficial.
fun_fact: Baby can yawn even though you can't feel movements yet.

title: The First Trimester is Ending
mother_changes: Uterus begins rising above the pelvis.
nutrition_tip: Maintain balanced meals rich in protein.
exercise_tip: Continue moderate activity if comfortable.
fun_fact: Baby already has fingerprints forming.

title: Welcome to the Second Trimester
mother_changes: Small baby bump may become noticeable.
nutrition_tip: Increase iron intake to support growing blood volume.
exercise_tip: Prenatal yoga may help improve flexibility.
fun_fact: Baby already has unique fingerprints.

title: Facial Expressions Develop
nutrition_tip: Eat fiber-rich foods to reduce constipation.
exercise_tip: Swimming is often a comfortable exercise.
fun_fact: Baby may suck their thumb.

title: Growing Stronger Every Day
nutrition_tip: Continue taking prenatal vitamins.
exercise_tip: Regular walking supports circulation.
fun_fact: Baby can detect light through the skin.

title: Muscles Become Stronger
baby_development: Heart pumps approximately 25 liters of blood daily.
mother_changes: Some mothers may begin feeling fluttering movements.
nutrition_tip: Increase calcium intake.
exercise_tip: Pelvic floor exercises can be helpful.
fun_fact: Baby's ears are close to their final position.

title: Body Fat Begins Forming
baby_development: Heartbeat can often be heard with a Doppler device.
nutrition_tip: Eat healthy fats like nuts and avocados.
exercise_tip: Prenatal stretching may relieve muscle tension.
fun_fact: Baby's fingerprints are now permanent.

title: Baby Can Hear Sounds
mother_changes: Sleep position may become less comfortable.
nutrition_tip: Drink plenty of water every day.
exercise_tip: Walking and swimming remain good options.
fun_fact: Baby may recognize your voice later in pregnancy.

title: Protective Skin Coating Forms
nutrition_tip: Include omega-3 rich foods if appropriate for your diet.
exercise_tip: Prenatal yoga may improve flexibility and relaxation.
fun_fact: The white coating on baby's skin helps protect it from amniotic fluid.

title: Halfway Through Pregnancy
mother_changes: The uterus reaches around the level of the belly button.
nutrition_tip: Continue a balanced diet with iron, calcium, and protein.
exercise_tip: Aim for regular moderate exercise if advised by your healthcare provider.
fun_fact: The anatomy scan is commonly performed around this time to assess development.
warning_signs: Reduced fetal movement after movements have become established

title: Baby Can Sense the World
baby_development: Eyebrows and eyelids are fully formed.
nutrition_tip: Eat foods rich in iron and vitamin C to support blood production.
exercise_tip: Swimming and walking are excellent low-impact exercises.
fun_fact: The flavors of foods you eat can slightly influence the taste of amniotic fluid.

title: Tiny Features Become Clear
baby_development: Lips, eyebrows and eyelashes are clearly visible.
Baby becomes more responsive to sounds.
mother_changes: Balance may change as your bump grows.
nutrition_tip: Stay hydrated and include calcium-rich foods.
exercise_tip: Maintain good posture during daily activities.
fun_fact: Baby may respond differently to familiar and unfamiliar voices.

title: Baby Can Hear Your Voice
mother_changes: Leg cramps can occur during sleep.
nutrition_tip: Magnesium-rich foods may help support muscle function.
exercise_tip: Gentle stretching before bed may reduce cramps.
fun_fact: Newborns often recognize their mother's voice immediately after birth.

title: Lungs Continue Developing
nutrition_tip: Eat smaller meals if heartburn occurs.
exercise_tip: Walking after meals may improve digestion.
fun_fact: Baby may become active at similar times every day.

title: Baby Responds to Touch
baby_development: Baby responds to touch and sound.
mother_changes: Sleeping comfortably may become more difficult.
nutrition_tip: Maintain adequate protein intake.
exercise_tip: Use pillows to support comfortable sleeping positions.
fun_fact: Baby may hiccup regularly as breathing muscles practice.

title: Eyes Begin Opening
mother_changes: Shortness of breath may occur occasionally.
nutrition_tip: Continue eating iron-rich foods daily.
exercise_tip: Take frequent breaks during physical activity.
fun_fact: Baby may blink inside the womb.

title: Welcome to the Third Trimester
nutrition_tip: Increase calcium and protein intake.
exercise_tip: Prenatal stretching may relieve back discomfort.
fun_fact: Baby now has regular sleep cycles.

title: Rapid Brain Development
mother_changes: Leg swelling may occur by evening.
nutrition_tip: Reduce salty processed foods if swelling is troublesome.
exercise_tip: Elevate your legs after prolonged standing.
fun_fact: Baby may dream during sleep.

title: Baby Gains Body Fat
baby_development: Baby regulates body temperature more effectively.
mother_changes: Baby kicks can feel quite powerful.
nutrition_tip: Choose smaller meals more frequently if heartburn occurs.
exercise_tip: Avoid lying flat on your back for long periods.
fun_fact: Baby's muscles are now strong enough to make visible movements from outside.

title: Preparing for Birth
baby_development: Bone marrow produces red blood cells.
mother_changes: Baby movements become stronger but space becomes tighter.
nutrition_tip: Continue eating iron-rich foods and staying well hydrated.
exercise_tip: Gentle walking and pelvic floor exercises remain beneficial if approved by your healthcare provider.
weekly_milestone: Baby continues preparing for life outside the womb.
fun_fact: Although movement patterns change as space decreases, your baby should still move regularly.

title: Baby's Senses Continue Developing
baby_development: Baby can distinguish light from darkness.
mother_changes: Braxton Hicks contractions may become more frequent.
nutrition_tip: Eat small, nutritious meals to meet increased energy needs.
exercise_tip: Continue gentle walking and pelvic floor exercises.
weekly_milestone: Baby's senses are becoming more refined.
fun_fact: Baby may recognize familiar voices and music.

title: Baby Practices Breathing
baby_development: Skin becomes smoother as fat increases.
nutrition_tip: Stay hydrated and continue iron-rich foods.
exercise_tip: Side sleeping with a pillow between the knees may improve comfort.
fun_fact: Most babies settle into a regular sleep pattern before birth.

title: Immune System Strengthens
baby_development: Bones harden while the skull remains flexible.
nutrition_tip: Continue a balanced diet with adequate protein.
exercise_tip: Avoid standing for long periods without breaks.
fun_fact: Baby's skull bones remain flexible to help during delivery.

title: Baby Continues Growing
mother_changes: Braxton Hicks contractions may become stronger.
nutrition_tip: Choose calcium-rich foods for bone health.
exercise_tip: Gentle stretching may ease muscle tightness.
weekly_milestone: Baby gains approximately 200–250 g each week.
fun_fact: Most babies spend much of their time sleeping now.

title: Baby's Kidneys Are Fully Developed
nutrition_tip: Eat protein-rich snacks between meals.
exercise_tip: Walking may help reduce stiffness.
weekly_milestone: Baby is nearly ready for birth.
fun_fact: Baby's hearing is fully developed and can recognize familiar voices.

title: Baby May Move Head Down
baby_development: Many babies move into a head-down position.
Lungs are close to full maturity.
mother_changes: Breathing may become easier if baby drops lower.
nutrition_tip: Continue eating nutrient-dense meals.
exercise_tip: Light walking remains beneficial unless advised otherwise.
weekly_milestone: Baby often settles into birth position.
fun_fact: Many babies now have a full head of hair, though this varies greatly.

title: Early Full Term
baby_development: Baby is considered early full term.
nutrition_tip: Stay hydrated and continue balanced meals.
exercise_tip: Short walks may improve comfort.
weekly_milestone: Baby is ready for birth if labor begins.
fun_fact: Many babies are born between weeks 37 and 40.

title: Baby Keeps Growing
baby_development: Baby practices coordinated sucking and swallowing.
nutrition_tip: Continue eating iron-rich foods.
exercise_tip: Listen to your body and rest when needed.
fun_fact: Baby's brain will continue developing rapidly after birth.

title: Full Term
baby_development: Lungs are ready for independent breathing.
Baby continues gaining a small amount of fat.
mother_changes: Labor can begin at any time.
nutrition_tip: Continue eating balanced meals and stay hydrated.
exercise_tip: Gentle walking may support comfort if approved by your healthcare provider.
fun_fact: Only about 5% of babies are born exactly on their estimated due date.

title: Meeting Your Baby Soon
baby_development: Baby is fully developed and ready for life outside the womb.
The lungs, brain and digestive system continue maturing.
Baby remains active while awaiting birth.
mother_changes: Pelvic pressure and contractions may increase.
nutrition_tip: Eat light, balanced meals and drink plenty of fluids.
exercise_tip: Stay mobile if comfortable and follow your healthcare team's guidance.
fun_fact: Many healthy pregnancies continue beyond 40 weeks under close medical supervision.
